cmes.note: CME visible to the west in STEREO A COR2. Not seen in GOES CCOR-1 or SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery. The potential source is opening field lines seen near or on the west limb in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery starting at 2026-07-13T00:35Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on potential source location in STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: Narrow CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-13T16:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 as it is likely obscured by the occulting disk. The source of this event is a very minor eruption seen along the NE limb in GOES SUVI 304 beginning at approx. 2026-07-13T15:45Z. Field line movement/opening is also visible in GOES SUVI 171 and 284.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky using a longitude of E90 due to source location along the NE limb and lack of visibility in COR2A to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-13T19:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 in later frames. This CME is not visible in STEREO A COR2 due to a data gap beginning at 2026-07-13T18:53Z. The source of this event is likely far sided as no clear eruptions are visible on the disk, but field line movement is visible from beyond the SE limb in GOES SUVI 284 beginning at approx. 2026-07-13T19:00Z. An estimated C9 flare is seen from preliminary Solar Orbiter STIX data, possibly from Active Region 14478, which rotated off of the Earth-facing disk around 2026-07-07. The approximate source longitude estimated from preliminary Solar Orbiter magnetogram data is S05W180.

cmeAnalyses.note: Updated measurement based on potential far-sided source location near AR 14478 as seen in preliminary Solar Orbiter data.
Updated shock measurement based on potential source location.
BULK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.
SHOCK measurement based on best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: CME visible to the SW in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 and to the SSW in STEREO A COR2. The potential source is a filament eruption seen gradually lifting off near the west limb as seen from GOES SUVI 304 imagery starting at 2026-07-14T02:18Z. The eruption may also be faintly seen on-disk in STEREO A EUV 195 imagery as dimming centered around S15W75.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery in SWPC_CAT.

cmes.note: CME visible to the east in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. The CME is not clearly visible in STEREO A COR2. The source is a C3.9 flare peaking at 2026-07-14T12:39Z from an unnumbered active region beyond the southeast limb as seen in SDO AIA imagery. Moving field lines are best seen in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 284 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the potential source location just beyond the east limb as seen in SDO AIA and GOES/SUVI imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1, and to the southwest in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is an eruption visible from beyond the western limb likely from former Active Region 14485 (~S10W110) starting around 2026-07-15T02:30Z as seen in SDO AIA 131/171/193/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/195/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery. An opening of field lines is visible over the southwest limb around this time in SDO AIA 171/193 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284 imagery. Additionally, dimming is observed from the eruption site starting around 2026-07-15T02:55Z seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 imagery.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1 imagery. This event is not observed in STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is unknown and likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth or STEREO A-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of 90 degrees due to the unknown source location.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SSE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-07-15T15:36Z. It is also seen to the SSE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. There is not a good source candidate for this CME on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is possibly far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of triangulation and lack of visible source candidate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SSE part way into the FOV of SOHO LASCO C2. It is also seen to the SSE in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1 but is covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The source is possibly related to a minor eruption seen on/near the far southern limb beginning around 2026-07-15T16:24Z in GOES SUVI 304.

cmeAnalyses.note: This measurement is at longitude E90 (not a plane-of-sky measurement) because the source appears to be right on the limb.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-07-15T17:48Z. This CME is also seen to the NW in GOES CCOR-1 but is obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3. It is also covered by a real-time data gap in STEREO A COR2. The only visible source candidate is some very minor, slow field line movement near the W limb best seen in GOES SUVI 284 as early as 2026-07-15T16:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement based on running difference from SOHO LASCO C2 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NW in SOHO LASCO C2 starting around 2026-07-15T21:24Z. It is only seen by SOHO LASCO C2 as it is mostly obscured by the pylon in SOHO LASCO C3 and is covered by data gaps in STEREO A COR2 and GOES CCOR-1. There is no source candidate on the Earth-facing disk.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of source candidate and single coronagraph view.

cmes.note: Faint and narrow CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-16T06:36Z. This CME fades before exiting the field-of-view in SOHO LASCO C2, and is therefore not visible in any other coronagraphs. The source of this event is minor field line movement seen along the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 and 193 beginning at approx. 2026-07-16T06:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of W90 due to source near the limb and lack of COR2A visibility to triangulate.

cmes.note: CME first seen to the SW by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-16T10:36Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This CME is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 due to an outage which began at 2026-07-15T20:00Z. The source of this event is an eruption from former Active Region 14485 (S10W130) beginning at approx. 2026-07-16T09:00Z as seen in STEREO A EUVI 195 and 304. This eruption was likely slightly deflected due to coronal hole proximity.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on approximate source location and best fit in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Bright loop CME first seen to the East by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-17T00:12Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 in later frames. This event is not seen in GOES CCOR-1 due to an outage which began at 2026-07-15T20:00Z. The source of this event is likely an eruption from beyond the East limb, evidenced by field line opening seen in SDO AIA 171, 193 and GOES SUVI 284 beginning at approx. 2026-07-17T23:30Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: BULK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.
SHOCK measurement based on approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: Faint CME first seen to the NE by SOHO LASCO C2 beginning at 2026-07-17T10:24Z, as well as by SOHO LASCO C3 in later frames. This CME is not clearly seen in STEREO A COR2 and is not visible in GOES CCOR-1 due to an outage which began at 2026-07-15T20:00Z. The source of this event is field line opening seen along the NE limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning at approx. 2026-07-17T09:12Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement using a longitude of E90 due to source location from beyond the NE limb and lack of visibility in other coronagraphs to triangulate.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption visible from beyond the southwest limb in SDO AIA 131/171/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery starting around 2026-07-17T17:35Z, possibly originating from former Active Region 14485 (S10W149). An opening of field lines is visible over the southwest limb in GOES SUVI 195 imagery at this time as well.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the southwest in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source may be related to an eruption visible from beyond the southwest limb in SDO AIA 131/171/304, GOES SUVI 131/171/284/304, and STEREO A EUVI 195/304 imagery starting around 2026-07-17T17:35Z, possibly originating from former Active Region 14485 (S10W149). An opening of field lines is visible over the southwest limb in GOES SUVI 195 imagery at this time as well. This event overlaps with CME: 2026-07-17T18:00Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the northeast in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 imagery for a few early frames before becoming too faint to observe. The source is likely far-sided as there are no source signatures on the Earth-facing disk around the time of this event.

cmeAnalyses.note: A plane-of-sky measurement was made using a longitude of -90 degrees due to the far-sided and therefore unknown source location.

cmes.note: Faint CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2, before being overtaken by brighter CME 2026-07-18T20:12Z. The source is not clearly seen separate from the eruption resulting in CME 2026-07-18T20:12Z, but may be a brief brightening in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 near former AR 14482 starting around 2026-07-18T18:45Z, located approximately around W105-W110.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: CME observed to the W in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2. This front quickly overtakes prior faint CME: 2026-07-18T19:36Z. The source of this CME is former AR 14482, observed best in STEREO A EUVI 195/304 as filament ejecta and brightening around approximately S08W105, starting around 2026-07-18T19:25Z. The eruption is observed over the West limb in SDO AIA 171/193/304 and GOES SUVI 171/195/284/304 as field line movement and the filament ejecta.

cmeAnalyses.note: Triangulation between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and GOES CCOR-1. In STEREO A COR2 imagery, however, this event is visible as a halo directed to the southeast. The source is likely a large dimming region centered near S03W35 starting around 2026-07-19T05:00Z seen in SDO AIA 193 and GOES SUVI 195/284 imagery. Western deflection is likely due to a large coronal hole east of the eruption site.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the approximate source location and best fit between SOHO LASCO C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: This CME is visible to the west in SOHO LASCO C2/C3, GOES CCOR-1, and STEREO A COR2 imagery. The source is likely related to an eruption from former Active Region 14482 (S09W120) seen in STEREO A EUVI 304 imagery starting around 2026-07-19T14:45Z.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on the best fit between SOHO LASCO C2/C3 and STEREO A COR2 imagery.

cmes.note: CME with an irregularly shaped leading edge first seen to the W in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-07-19T21:24Z. This CME is also seen to the W in STEREO A COR2 but disappears before leaving the FOV of STEREO A COR2. It is very faint and difficult to track in both SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. The source of this CME is beyond the SW limb. Opening field lines and rising filamentary material can been seen over the SW limb in SDO AIA 171 beginning as early as 2026-07-19T19:30Z. There may be two distinct portions of the ejecta creating two features in coronagraphs; however, the greater leading edge appears to move together, so this has been treated as a single event. The eruption can be seen in STEREO A EUVI 304 from former AR 14482 (S09W121) beginning around 2026-07-19T20:15Z due to the instrument cadence.

cmeAnalyses.note: Measurement based on best fit between SOHO LASCO C2 and STEREO A COR2 only. The leading edge is too faint/diffuse to track in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1.

cmes.note: Faint, narrow, jet-like CME first seen to the NE in SOHO LASCO C2 starting at 2026-07-19T23:00Z. It is also seen for early frames in SOHO LASCO C3 and GOES CCOR-1. No good source candidate has been found on the Earth-facing disk, so the source is most likely far-sided.

cmeAnalyses.note: Plane-of-sky measurement chosen due to lack of triangulation and lack of source candidate.
